sentiment.rationale: Addresses the Missouri persecutions with divine theodicy—saints afflicted because of transgressions but Zion will be redeemed; includes the parable of the nobleman and the olive trees.
Minutes of the formation of the first high council—procedural, organizational, and peaceful in establishing church governance.
Calls the saints to gather strength (Zion's Camp) to redeem Zion, with sharp warnings about disobedience preventing redemption.
Reorganizes the united order with specific stewardship assignments, stern warnings about covenant-breaking, and promises for the faithful.
Reveals why Zion could not be redeemed—the saints were not united or obedient—and commands them to wait for endowment before attempting redemption.
Brief call to Warren Cowdery as presiding high priest in Freedom, New York—encouraging and affirming.
Grand revelation on priesthood organization—Melchizedek and Aaronic, quorums, patriarchal order, keys from Adam to Moses—foundational and instructive.
Brief, encouraging revelation to Lyman Sherman promising strength and a place among the righteous.
The Kirtland Temple dedicatory prayer—sublime petition for divine acceptance, endowment of power, mercy for Zion, and gathering of Israel.
Christ appears in the Kirtland Temple; Moses, Elias, and Elijah restore keys—one of the most glorious visions in the Restoration.
The Lord reassures Joseph in Salem about temporal treasures and promises to bless their efforts despite concerns about debt.
Commission to Thomas B. Marsh as president of the Twelve, with stern warnings about humility and a declaration that judgments begin at God's house.
Brief Q&A interpreting Isaiah passages about the stem, rod, and root of Jesse—doctrinal and interpretive.
Two-verse revelation calling David W. Patten to prepare for a mission the following spring.
Reveals the name of the Church (The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints), designates Far West as a stake of Zion, and commands temple construction.
Single verse identifying Spring Hill, Missouri as Adam-ondi-Ahman—peaceful, prophetic designation.
Rebukes William Marks and Newel K. Whitney for covetousness, and Oliver Granger is commended for faithfulness.
Calls new members to the Twelve and sets the date for their mission departure from Far West.
Establishes the law of tithing—one-tenth of interest annually—as a standing law forever.
Single verse establishing the council for disposition of tithing funds—brief and administrative.

summaries.summary: The Lord explains why the saints were driven from Zion—transgression and failure to share equally—promises eventual redemption, and teaches the parable of the nobleman and the olive trees.
Minutes of the organization of the first high council of the church at Kirtland, establishing procedures for church trials and the council's authority.
The Lord calls the saints to gather strength and redeem Zion, paralleling their effort to Moses leading Israel, but warns Zion cannot be redeemed without obedience.
The Lord reorganizes the united order with specific stewardship assignments to named individuals, promising blessings to the faithful and destruction to covenant-breakers.
The Lord reveals that Zion cannot be redeemed yet because the saints were not united or obedient, and commands them to wait for the endowment of power in Kirtland.
A brief call to Warren A. Cowdery as presiding high priest in Freedom, New York, with encouragement to be faithful and promises of blessings.
Grand revelation on priesthood organization: Melchizedek and Aaronic orders, quorum structures, the patriarchal priesthood from Adam, and the duty of presiding officers.
A brief revelation to Lyman Sherman assuring him his sins are forgiven, calling him to strengthen his brethren, and promising a place among the righteous.
Joseph Smith's dedicatory prayer for the Kirtland Temple, petitioning for divine acceptance, endowment of power, mercy on Zion, and the gathering of scattered Israel.
Christ appears in the Kirtland Temple accepting it, followed by Moses restoring gathering keys, Elias committing the Abrahamic dispensation, and Elijah restoring sealing keys.
The Lord reassures Joseph Smith in Salem, Massachusetts that there are temporal and spiritual treasures in the city and His purposes will be accomplished.
The Lord commissions Thomas B. Marsh as president of the Twelve Apostles, commands humility, warns about pride, and declares that judgment shall begin at God's house.
Questions and answers interpreting Isaiah's prophecies about the stem, rod, and root of Jesse, identifying them as Christ, a latter-day servant, and a descendant of Jesse.
A two-verse revelation calling David W. Patten to settle his business and prepare for a mission with the Twelve the following spring.
The Lord reveals the official name of the Church—The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints—designates Far West as a stake of Zion, and commands a temple there.
A single verse identifying Spring Hill, Daviess County, Missouri as Adam-ondi-Ahman, the place where Adam shall visit his people.
The Lord rebukes William Marks and Newel K. Whitney for covetousness, commands them to settle in Far West, and commends Oliver Granger for his faithfulness.
The Lord calls new members to fill vacancies in the Quorum of the Twelve and sets April 26, 1839 as the departure date for their mission from Far West.
The Lord establishes the law of tithing, requiring the saints to pay one-tenth of their interest annually as a standing law forever.
A single verse establishing that tithing funds shall be disposed of by a council of the First Presidency, bishops, and high council.

crossReferences.note: The parable of the nobleman and the olive trees echoes Isaiah's vineyard song about Israel.
The Adam-ondi-Ahman council where Adam blesses his posterity parallels Daniel's Ancient of Days vision.
Joseph petitions for the Spirit to be poured out upon all flesh, echoing Joel's prophecy.
Christ's sudden appearance in the Kirtland Temple fulfills 'the Lord, whom ye seek, shall suddenly come to his temple.'
Elijah's appearance fulfills the prophecy of his return before the great and dreadful day, and D&C 2.
Both establish that the church must be called by Christ's name to be His church.
Both teach the principle of tithing—returning a portion to the Lord—with promises of blessing.

verses.reason: 'I, the Lord God, make you free, therefore ye are free indeed; and the law also maketh you free'—constitutional liberty theology.
'They must needs be chastened and tried, even as Abraham'—explaining why the saints suffered persecution.
'Zion shall be redeemed, although she is chastened for a little season.'
'Zion cannot be built up unless it is by the principles of the law of the celestial kingdom'—defines Zion's requirements.
'Mine elders should wait for a little season for the redemption of Zion.'
'Let every man learn his duty, and to act in the office in which he is appointed, in all diligence'—widely quoted in LDS leadership.
Adam gathers his posterity at Adam-ondi-Ahman and bestows his last blessing—a foundational LDS eschatological event.
'Seek ye out of the best books words of wisdom; seek learning, even by study and also by faith'—repeated from D&C 88:118, embedded in the temple prayer.
'May all people who shall enter upon the threshold of the Lord's house may feel thy power, and feel constrained to acknowledge that thou hast sanctified it.'
'The veil was taken from our minds, and the eyes of our understanding were opened'—describes the beginning of the Kirtland Temple vision.
'I am the first and the last; I am he who liveth, I am he who was slain; I am your advocate with the Father'—Christ's self-identification.
Elijah commits the keys of this dispensation, fulfilling Malachi's prophecy—the turning of hearts of fathers and children.
'Be thou humble; and the Lord thy God shall lead thee by the hand, and give thee answer to thy prayers'—widely quoted counsel on humility.
Reveals the official name: 'The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints'—the definitive naming revelation.
Identifies Spring Hill as Adam-ondi-Ahman—a key LDS prophetic site tied to Adam's return.
'This shall be the beginning of the tithing of my people... one-tenth of all their interest annually'—establishes the law of tithing.
